name: fetch
description: Secure HTTPS-only fetch for sandboxed JavaScript. Default-deny domain allowlist, SSRF protection, rate limiting, and audit logging.

hints.overview: HTTP(S) fetch for external data. All calls return synchronously.
criticalRules: Only allowlisted domains work — non-allowlisted = blocked permanently
fetchJSON(url) for JSON APIs, fetchText(url) for HTML/text, fetchBinary(url) for images
fetchBinaryBatch([urls]) for multiple downloads — does NOT throw, check each result.error
Check rateLimit.remaining on responses — stop if 0
Use head(url) to check content type before downloading
antiPatterns: Don't use fetchJSON on non-JSON URLs (HTML, raw text) — use fetchText instead
Don't retry permanent errors (domain not allowlisted, POST not allowed, content type blocked)
Don't use manual read loops — use fetchText/fetchBinary/fetchBinaryBatch convenience methods
commonPatterns: Web page: const html = fetchText(url); const {text, links} = parseHtml(html)
Batch images: const results = fetchBinaryBatch([url1, url2, url3])
